evals.prompt: Run one autoresearch cycle on my Obsidian wiki and improve the highest-value gap you find.
evals.expected_output: Selects one wiki gap, chooses a bounded cycle type, records before/after health signals, applies or drafts changes safely, and reports a keep/discard decision.
expectations: Initializes or reads _system/autoresearch cycle memory before selecting a gap.
Uses score_candidates.mjs or an equivalent explicit scoring pass before choosing the candidate.
Chooses one cycle type and one reviewable target rather than mixing multiple broad maintenance tasks.
Captures a before/after scorecard with benchmark questions when the cycle is research or synthesis.
Records the cycle decision and rationale in the autoresearch ledger.
Updates the agenda with useful next candidates or blocked lessons.

evals.prompt: Use my raw clippings backlog to improve the wiki for a while, but keep it reviewable.
evals.expected_output: Treats this as a budgeted backlog distillation run, limits scope to a small related batch, preserves provenance, updates tracking files, and summarizes changes.
expectations: Uses a finite budget appropriate to 'for a while' and does not promise indefinite operation.
Selects a small related raw-source batch instead of ingesting the whole backlog blindly.
Preserves source paths or URLs in page frontmatter or source summaries.
Adds or reuses benchmark questions for the distilled topic when the result should improve retrieval.
Records high-value source-backed claims in the source-claim map when claims are subtle, disputed, or likely to go stale.
Writes a ledger entry with pages changed, sources added, keep/discard decision, and lessons.

evals.prompt: Can you run an overnight autoresearch loop on the wiki?
evals.expected_output: Sets a finite overnight budget, explains safety checkpoints, uses wiki skills in cycles, and stops with a reviewable report rather than promising infinite execution.
expectations: Sets the default overnight budget to eight cycles unless the user specifies otherwise.
Performs or explains the git branch or backup safety checkpoint before vault edits.
Uses the candidate scorer and prior ledger entries to avoid repeating recently discarded candidates.
Records each cycle separately in the ledger rather than only producing a final prose summary.
Turns discarded or quarantined cycles into lessons, blocked items, or agenda entries.
Stops with a concise report covering kept changes, discarded changes, health delta, and autoresearch memory updates.

evals.prompt: Run one autoresearch cycle, and prioritize pages that have open questions or conflicting statements.
evals.expected_output: Runs the bundled uncertainty scanner or an equivalent deterministic marker search, checks whether the answer already exists in the wiki or raw source collections, calls wiki-research when external evidence is needed, and updates the originating pages with a sourced resolution or a clear unresolved state.
expectations: Runs find_uncertainty_markers.mjs or score_candidates.mjs and prioritizes concrete open questions or conflicts.
Checks existing wiki and raw-source collections before using external research.
Captures competing claims, scopes, dates, and provenance when handling a conflict.
Updates the originating page with either a sourced resolution or an explicit unresolved state.
Records conflict-related claims in source-claims.jsonl through the cycle record.
Adds or reuses benchmark questions that test whether the conflict or open question is now easier to answer.

evals.prompt: Continue autoresearch from the last run and choose the next best cycle.
evals.expected_output: Uses prior cycle memory, agenda items, benchmark questions, source-claim map entries, and discarded-cycle lessons to choose the next bounded improvement.
expectations: Reads recent cycles.jsonl entries before selecting the next candidate.
Uses agenda.md as a positive signal for promising next cycles.
Penalizes or reframes recently discarded or quarantined candidates instead of repeating them.
Reuses benchmark questions from benchmark-questions.json when they fit the selected topic.
Treats disputed, stale, or unverified source-claim map entries as high-value research leads.
Records the new cycle so the next continuation has fresh memory.
